findings.description: Cell A3 in xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml has font color #FFFFFF on the default white fill. The text is invisible to a human reading the spreadsheet but is preserved in the cell's inline-string and read by every downstream extractor including CSV exports and LLM ingestion.

inversion_recovery.concealed: color=#FFFFFF; recovered text: 'HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex'

findings.description: Cell A3 formula in xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml contains a HYPERLINK to an external URL with a 55-character display text. The display text is rendered as the cell label while the URL drives the click target; long display text is structurally suitable for carrying a payload that automated consumers read but human viewers do not interpret.

inversion_recovery.concealed: url='http://attacker.example/exfil'; display: 'HIDDEN_TEXT_PAYLOAD: actual revenue $10,000 - see annex'

findings.description: Cell A4 formula in xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml matches shell-trigger pattern 'cmd_pipe'. Legacy Excel honours such formulas as external application calls when the workbook is opened (subject to user prompts in modern Excel). Never legitimate in a content-bearing spreadsheet.
